perturbation.original_text: (11) Governing Law . This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the Commonwealth of Massachusetts applicable to agreements made and to be performed entirely within Massachusetts.
perturbation.changed_text: (1) Confidential Information . The term “Confidential Information” also shall be deemed to include the portion of all notes, analyses, compilations, studies, interpretations or other documents prepared by Recipient or its Representatives that contain Confidential Information. The term “Confidential Information” shall not include information that (i) is or becomes generally available to the public other than as a result of a disclosure by Recipient or its Representatives, (ii) was within Recipient’s possession prior to its being furnished to it by or on behalf of the Disclosing Party pursuant hereto, provided that the source of such information was not known by Recipient to be bound by a confidentiality agreement with, or other contractual, legal or fiduciary obligation of confidentiality to, the Disclosing Party with respect to such information, (iii) is independently developed by the Recipient or (iv) becomes available to Recipient on a non-confidential basis from a source other than the Disclosing Party or any of its Representatives, provided that such source is not bound by a confidentiality agreement with, or other contractual, legal or fiduciary obligation of confidentiality to, the Disclosing Party or any other party with respect to such information.(11) Governing Law . This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the Commonwealth of Massachusetts applicable to agreements made and to be performed entirely within Massachusetts.
perturbation.explanation: Moving the governing law clause into the 'Confidential Information' definition makes it harder to find and could lead to disputes about which jurisdiction's laws apply, affecting enforceability.
perturbation.law_explanation: M.G.L. c. 93A, § 2 requires fair and conspicuous disclosure of important terms. Burying the governing law within a definition section obfuscates it, potentially violating this requirement by making it less accessible to a reasonable person.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: General Law - Part I, Title XV, Chapter 93A, Section 2 Skip to Content General Laws Part I ADMINISTRATION OF THE GOVERNMENT Title XV REGULATION OF TRADE Chapter 93A REGULATION OF BUSINESS PRACTICES FOR CONSUMERS PROTECTION Section 2 Unfair practices; legislative intent; rules and regulations Section 2 Search Search the Legislature Search General Laws Go Directly to a General Law Chapter Section Go to General Law Skip to Content Print Page Prev ious Next Section 2: Unfair practices; legislative intent; rules and regulations Section 2. (a) Unfair methods of competition and unfair or deceptive acts or practices in the conduct of any trade or commerce are hereby declared unlawful. (b) It is the intent of the legislature that in construing paragraph (a) of this section in actions brought under sections four, nine and eleven, the courts will be guided by the interpretations given by the Federal Trade Commission and the Federal Courts to section 5(a)(1) of the Federal Trade Commission Act (15 U.S.C. 45(a)(1)), as from time to time amended. (c) The attorney general may make rules and regulations interpreting the provisions of subsection 2(a) of this chapter. Such rules and regulations shall not be inconsistent with the rules, regulations and decisions of the Federal Trade Commission and the Federal Courts interpreting the provisions of 15 U.S.C. 45(a)(1) (The Federal Trade Commission Act), as from time to time amended. × Register for MyLegislature Register With An Existing Account Sign in with Facebook Sign in with Google Register With Your Email Address Email Address Password Confirm Password Register Registering... perturbation.justification: The perturbation explanation states that moving the governing law clause makes it harder to find and could lead to disputes, affecting enforceability. The law explanation states that M.G.L. c. 93A, § 2 requires fair and conspicuous disclosure of important terms and burying the governing law within a definition section obfuscates it. Scraped law snippets 1 and 2 both refer to M.G.L. c. 93A, § 2 regarding unfair or deceptive acts or practices. The 'Structural Flaws Legal Contradiction' definition states that a contradiction arises when a required legal disclosure is placed in an irrelevant or misleading section, making it difficult to locate or interpret. The original text clearly presents the governing law, while the changed text buries it within the 'Confidential Information' section. This obfuscation of a crucial term constitutes a structural flaw that contradicts the principle of fair disclosure, therefore a legal contradiction exists.
